items.title: Teen who allegedly stormed plane with shotgun to face adult court

children.content: A Victorian teenager who allegedly tried to hijack a plane has failed in a bid to keep his case in the Children's Court, where the maximum penalty is four years in jail.

gallery.altText: Several people holding down a teenage boy in an aeroplane

thumbnail.altText: Several people holding down a teenage boy in an aeroplane

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Armed police respond to siege in Melbourne's inner west

children.content: Neighbours waited anxiously on Friday morning until the siege was resolved. Victoria Police said it took about two hours for the man to surrender.

image.altText: Police officer wearing tactical gear at the top of an armoured vehicle.

items.title: Bulldogs erase 29-point deficit to claim spiteful thriller against Hawks

children.content: The Western Bulldogs kick eight goals to one in the second half to overrun Hawthorn in a thriller at the MCG.

image.altText: A team of AFL players in blue and red gather and tussle with a player in yellow and black

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery poses risk if granted bail, court hears

children.content: Zeinab Ahmad, who recently returned to Australia, is seeking bail after she was charged with slavery offences after allegedly crossing into Syria with her family in 2015.

image.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: No place like home with rescuers for Dorothy the kitten who survived the unthinkable

children.content: A kitten left for dead after being thrown from a moving car has spent months recovering and is now at a new home with two familiar faces.

image.altText: A grey kitten with yellow eyes, lying ina  bed, with a bandage around one of its legs.

items.title: Victoria's top cop on when the scourge of firebombings will stop

children.content: Criminals in the construction sector, youth offending and station staffing are discussed in a wide-ranging interview with Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush.

articles.title: Properties searched during investigation into Melbourne arson attacks

children.content: Police seize a tomahawk, cash, steroids, illicit tobacco, mobile phones and other devices while searching seven properties this morning, following ongoing attacks at Melbourne hospitality venues.

image.altText: Fire engines parked in a street at night

gallery.altText: Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush

thumbnail.altText: Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush

items.title: Former judge facing new child sex charges

children.content: 75-year-old Alan Boulton is accused of travelling outside Australia and engaging in sexual activity with a child under 16 a decade ago, according to charge sheets.

image.altText: A photo of the Monash University law chambers in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: CCTV vision shows teens' brutal attack on homeless man

children.content: Police say the man stumbled into a sports bar with stab wounds and collapsed.

image.altText: CCTV of a street at night time, group of four walking down the road.

items.title: Victoria 'failing the whole nation' in enforcement of anti-vaping measures

children.content: Anti-vaping advocates have launched a new campaign warning young people of the health dangers posed by vaping. They say laws in Victoria are not helping their cause.

image.altText: A hand holding a purple vape, Bunbury tower in the background

items.title: Melbourne Storm boss hails 'positive' talks with Tasmania to bring games south

children.content: Melbourne Storm chief executive Justin Rodski says he's "got no doubt" a deal with Tasmania can be reached if the commercial interests line up, following a round of courting from premier Jeremy Rockliff.

image.altText: Cameron Munster of the Storm looks to pass the ball

items.title: Families demand answers as truck bypass threatens homes

children.content: Landholders in Victoria's east say a proposed $21-million freight bypass could carve through their properties and transform their rural lives, while the government defends the route as essential for trucks.

gallery.altText: A photo of a man wearing a black jumper and cap,  next to a woman wearing a white jumper, standing in a peaceful backyard

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a man wearing a black jumper and cap,  next to a woman wearing a white jumper, standing in a peaceful backyard

items.title: Family and community in limbo two months on from violent death

children.content: The family of Richard Wills, whose body was found in Victoria's north-west at Easter, say police have not kept them updated on the investigation.

image.altText: mad wearing black cap and blue jumper

items.title: Neo-Nazi hecklers found guilty over Anzac Day booing

children.content: Three white supremacists who loudly booed during a Welcome to Country ceremony at an Anzac Day dawn service in Melbourne have been found guilty of offensive behaviour and ordered to pay fines.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit and white shirt carrying a book walks outside a court room.

items.title: Failings of Victoria's child protection system including 35 deaths laid bare

children.content: An inquiry by the Commission for Children and Young People finds children are falling through the cracks of a state system under overwhelming pressure.

image.altText: The silhouette of a child sitting on a bed with an adult sitting alongside them.

items.title: Fewer cars stolen across Australia — except in Victoria

children.content: Victoria has higher vehicle insurance claims over stolen cars than all other mainland states combined, new data from the Insurance Council of Australia reveals.

image.altText: Attempted Tallangatta car theft

items.title: Court hears 'ISIS bride' mistreated a woman her family enslaved

children.content: Prosecutors have argued Zeinab Ahmad actively participated in the mistreatment of a woman her family enslaved in Syria.

gallery.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

thumbnail.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: Hot spot for Australian bull semen as China market reopens

children.content: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

image.altText: Photo of vials of frozen bull semen.

items.title: Breeder withdraws proposal for controversial Victorian puppy farm

children.content: An application for a proposed puppy farm in central Victoria has been withdrawn after lobbying by nearby residents and animal activists.

image.altText: Australian shepherd dogs behind a cage with one dog looing through a hole in the fence.

items.title: 'Not enough' bees for crop pollination as parasite takes over

children.content: A new report warns of a massive pollination shortfall in Australia this year as varroa mite wipes out hives and forces beekeepers out.

image.altText: A honey bee collecting pollen from an almond blossom.

items.title: Six charged over death and alleged kidnapping in Melbourne's west

children.content: Police raided several properties yesterday after the body of a man was found near a children's playground in Tarneit last month.

image.altText: A man in handcuffs being led by police.

items.title: Child sex offender given 'disability pride' award after admin error

children.content: Repeat child sex offender Cameron Bloomfield was crowned as Victoria's "disability pride champion" in 2024, after a government department admin error.

gallery.altText: Cameron Bloomfield stands outside in dappled light, smiling at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: Cameron Bloomfield stands outside in dappled light, smiling at the camera.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Underworld figure Mick Gatto released without charge following arrest

children.content: Mick Gatto has been released without charge after being arrested by Victorian police investigating "alleged financial offences" earlier today.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat and bejewelled chain sits among a crowd

items.title: John Setka faces court over alleged threatening messages

children.content: Former construction union boss John Setka has faced court fighting allegations he sent abusive emails, with his lawyer saying he has received threats on his life.

image.altText: The CFMEU's Victoria secretary, John Setka, addresses construction workers in 2013.

items.title: 'Us duck hunters': Victorian watchdog accused of siding with shooters

children.content: The Game Management Authority is accused of turning a blind eye to illegal behaviour by shooters while bullying wildlife rescuers and anti-hunting campaigners.

image.altText: A man with a shotgun over his shoulder carrying a gun in his other hand, up to his knees in water

items.title: Coalition declares Labor's changes to Vic election laws 'unconstitutional'

children.content: Six months out from the state election, Victoria had no rules governing donations due to the High Court finding in April that the laws were unconstitutional.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan points while speaking in parliament.

items.title: Sex offender evicted after women footballers abandon bush club

children.content: A south-west Victorian football club has apologised after it allowed a convicted sex offender to return to the club after a stint in jail.

gallery.altText: A Tyrendarra Football Netball Club training jumper can be seen on a timber bench.

thumbnail.altText: A Tyrendarra Football Netball Club training jumper can be seen on a timber bench.

items.title: Neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell broadcasting hate on Instagram from Victorian acreage

children.content: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell has been uploading antisemitic and homophobic diatribes to Instagram from a property on the outskirts of Melbourne.

image.altText: On the left, a man wearing a sparkling medieval mask. On the right a bald man with a moustache on the same background

items.title: Luke Sayers loses battle to have lewd photo case heard in private

children.content: A bid by Mr Sayers to have a defamation case brought by his wife over a nude photo scandal heard privately has been rejected by a judge.

image.altText: A bald man sits alone at a long table with a microphone. He is wearing a black suit, white collared shirt and purple tie.

items.title: Duo arrested in north-west Victoria after cross-border pursuit

children.content: Victorian police say two people have been arrested over an alleged crime spree and cross-border pursuit in the Sunraysia region.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Man charged over weekend double stabbing across Melbourne

children.content: The 27-year-old is accused of two separate stabbings on the opposite sides of Melbourne.

image.altText: Two men hold the arms of a shirtless man in grey pants with his hands handcuffed behind him near a white van.

items.title: These companies have controversial pasts. Google AI didn’t tell us

children.content: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

gallery.altText: A hand holding a phone where the user has googled tobacco company Philip Morris.

thumbnail.altText: A hand holding a phone where the user has googled tobacco company Philip Morris.

items.title: Media mogul Antony Catalano has bail varied to allow travel to Byron Bay

children.content: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano will be allowed to visit his wife and kids after successfully applying to have his bail conditions changed.

image.altText: A smiling businessman

items.title: Changes to Victorian IVF laws introduced after Monash IVF bungles

children.content: The government is introducing amendments to laws governing IVF clinics that will allow the state's health minister to personally intervene to cancel a provider's registration.

image.altText: An IVF procedure

items.title: Police release CCTV footage of Melbourne shooting incident

children.content: A man was filmed firing three shots at a house in Melbourne's north in what police believe was a targeted incident last year.

image.altText: A man pointing a gun on CCTV.

items.title: Parent 'thought it was a joke' upon hearing year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen

children.content: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.

image.altText: two school girls write in a book during class.
